summary: The parallel test test-debugger-exec failed because a test helper timed out waiting for the debugger's initial break message.
root_cause: The test helper waitForInitialBreak timed out after 15000ms while waiting for the expected debugger output (/break (?:on start )?in/i), causing parallel/test-debugger-exec.js to fail.

evidence.quote: Error: Timeout (15000) while waiting for /break (?:on start )?in/i
evidence.reason: Shows the test helper threw a timeout error waiting for the specified regex, which is the immediate cause of the failure.
Indicates the timeout occurred inside the waitForInitialBreak helper in test/common/debugger.js.
Shows the failing test is parallel/test-debugger-exec.js and that it invoked waitInitialBreak which triggered the timeout.
The debug output shows the inspector started and was attached, but the expected break message did not appear in the captured output.
Confirms the exact command used to run the failing test.

suggested_fix: Run the failing test locally with the same command to reproduce: out/Release/node /Users/runner/work/node/node/node/test/parallel/test-debugger-exec.js. Investigate why the debugger did not emit the expected initial break message (check test timing, inspector behavior, or changes to output). As a short-term mitigation, consider increasing the timeout (currently 15000ms) in test/common/debugger.js or adjust the test to wait for the actual observed prompt (e.g. 'debug> ').
